root_cause_hypothesis: Hard-link dcache updates overwrite the canonical inode path so subsequent linkat calls resolve the wrong source path (ENOENT), and link/symlink FUSE handlers skip POSIX permission and sticky-directory checks that LTP expects when check_permission is enabled.
planned_changes: Stop rewriting inode parent/name on hard link so get_path keeps a valid source path.
Add parent write/search and sticky hard-link permission checks before link RPC.
Add parent write/search permission checks before symlink RPC.
Add unit tests for dcache path stability and permission helpers.
not_goals: Do not change master-side link/symlink persistence semantics.
Do not refactor unrelated rename or setattr paths.
Do not alter harness runner configuration.
verification_plan: Harness LTP linkat01, prot_hsymlinks, link01 on curvine-fuse

risk.rationale: Aligns FUSE link/symlink with POSIX permission semantics already enforced on other mutating ops; dcache path fix is localized to hard-link bookkeeping.
